summary: The monolithic @modelcontextprotocol/sdk package is retired in v2 and split into @modelcontextprotocol/server, @modelcontextprotocol/client, @modelcontextprotocol/core (wire schemas), and runtime adapters (@modelcontextprotocol/node, express, hono, fastify). v2 is ESM-only, requires Node.js 20+, and requires Zod ^4.2.0 (Zod v3 schemas fail silently at registration). The variadic server.tool()/prompt()/resource() methods are removed in favor of registerTool()/registerPrompt()/registerResource() config objects using Standard Schema. The handler `extra` parameter is restructured into a `ctx` object (ctx.mcpReq / ctx.http). setRequestHandler/setNotificationHandler take method strings instead of Zod request schemas. McpError is renamed ProtocolError; OAuth error classes are consolidated into OAuthError + OAuthErrorCode. SSEServerTransport and WebSocketClientTransport are removed. Separately from the package upgrade, v2 can OPT IN to the 2026-07-28 stateless protocol revision (no initialize handshake, no Mcp-Session-Id, server/discover probing, inputRequired() replacing sampling/elicitation push); upgrading the SDK alone does NOT change the wire protocol. An official codemod automates most of the mechanical migration: npx @modelcontextprotocol/codemod@beta v1-to-v2 . — v1.x remains supported with bug fixes and security updates for at least 6 months after v2 ships (stable expected 2026-07-28).

breaking_changes.title: Package split: @modelcontextprotocol/sdk is retired
breaking_changes.description: The single @modelcontextprotocol/sdk package splits into focused packages: @modelcontextprotocol/server (servers), @modelcontextprotocol/client (clients), @modelcontextprotocol/core (shared Zod wire schemas), @modelcontextprotocol/node (Node HTTP transport), and framework adapters @modelcontextprotocol/express / hono / fastify. Deep subpath imports (…/server/mcp.js, …/client/index.js) are replaced by package barrels and short subpaths.
breaking_changes.migration_note: Replace the @modelcontextprotocol/sdk dependency with @modelcontextprotocol/server and/or @modelcontextprotocol/client. Server code imports from @modelcontextprotocol/server; client code from @modelcontextprotocol/client; shared *Schema constants from @modelcontextprotocol/core. The official codemod rewrites import paths automatically: npx @modelcontextprotocol/codemod@beta v1-to-v2 .

breaking_changes.title: ESM-only, Node.js 20+ required
breaking_changes.description: v2 packages ship as ESM only and require Node.js 20+, Bun, or Deno. CommonJS require() of the SDK no longer works.
breaking_changes.migration_note: Ensure the consuming project runs on Node 20+ and can load ESM ("type": "module" in package.json, or import from an ESM context). Projects stuck on CJS or Node <20 must stay on v1.x, which remains supported with bug fixes and security updates for at least 6 months after v2 ships.

breaking_changes.title: Zod v3 no longer supported — requires zod ^4.2.0
breaking_changes.description: v2 depends on zod ^4.2.0. Zod v3 can still be installed alongside, but v3 schemas fail SILENTLY at runtime when registered as tool/prompt schemas — no compile error, no throw at registration.
breaking_changes.migration_note: Upgrade zod to ^4.2.0 BEFORE upgrading the SDK. This failure mode is dangerous: with Zod v3 still installed, schema registration appears to succeed but validation is broken at runtime. Audit every z.* schema passed to registerTool/registerPrompt for Zod v4 compatibility.

breaking_changes.title: server.tool()/prompt()/resource() variadic methods removed
breaking_changes.description: The variadic registration methods server.tool(), server.prompt(), and server.resource() are removed. Registration uses registerTool()/registerPrompt()/registerResource() with a single config object (description, inputSchema, etc.).
breaking_changes.migration_note: Move positional description/schema arguments into the config object. Note the schema becomes a full z.object(...) rather than a raw shape (see the Standard Schema entry). The codemod handles the mechanical rewrite.

breaking_changes.title: Tool/prompt schemas use Standard Schema — raw Zod shapes deprecated
breaking_changes.description: inputSchema/outputSchema accept any Standard Schema-compliant object (Zod v4, Valibot, ArkType). Passing a raw shape like { name: z.string() } still works via @deprecated overloads but is slated for removal.
breaking_changes.migration_note: Wrap raw shapes in z.object(...). This also unlocks non-Zod validators (Valibot, ArkType) since v2 targets the Standard Schema interface.

breaking_changes.title: Handler context `extra` restructured into `ctx` (ctx.mcpReq / ctx.http)
breaking_changes.description: The second handler parameter is renamed extra → ctx and remapped: extra.signal → ctx.mcpReq.signal; extra.requestId → ctx.mcpReq.id; extra.sendRequest() → ctx.mcpReq.send(); extra.sendNotification() → ctx.mcpReq.notify(); extra.requestInfo → ctx.http?.req (web-standard Request); extra.authInfo → ctx.http?.authInfo; extra.closeSSEStream/closeStandaloneSSEStream → ctx.http?.closeSSE()/closeStandaloneSSE(). ctx.http is optional (undefined on stdio).
breaking_changes.migration_note: Protocol-level members live under ctx.mcpReq; HTTP-level members under the OPTIONAL ctx.http (guard with ?. — it is undefined on stdio). The experimental task members (extra.taskStore, extra.taskId, extra.taskRequestedTtl) are removed entirely (see experimental tasks entry). The RequestHandlerExtra<TReq, TNotif> type is replaced by ServerContext/ClientContext without type parameters.

breaking_changes.title: setRequestHandler/setNotificationHandler take method strings, not Zod schemas
breaking_changes.description: Spec handlers are registered by method string ('tools/call') instead of a request schema constant (CallToolRequestSchema). Custom (non-spec) methods use a 3-argument form with explicit { params, result } schemas.
breaking_changes.migration_note: Replace each schema constant with its wire method string. For custom methods, pass { params, result } as the second argument; the handler then receives parsed params directly.

breaking_changes.title: McpError renamed to ProtocolError; ErrorCode to ProtocolErrorCode
breaking_changes.description: The protocol-level JSON-RPC error class McpError is renamed ProtocolError, and the ErrorCode enum is renamed ProtocolErrorCode. A new SdkError + SdkErrorCode pair covers SDK-local failures (RequestTimeout, ConnectionClosed, InvalidResult).
breaking_changes.before_code:
import { McpError, ErrorCode } from '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/types.js';

throw new McpError(ErrorCode.InvalidParams, 'bad input');
breaking_changes.after_code:
import { ProtocolError, ProtocolErrorCode } from '@modelcontextprotocol/core';

throw new ProtocolError(ProtocolErrorCode.InvalidParams, 'bad input');
breaking_changes.migration_note: Rename McpError → ProtocolError and ErrorCode → ProtocolErrorCode everywhere. Catch-blocks that distinguish transport/SDK failures should also check the new SdkError/SdkErrorCode.

breaking_changes.title: StreamableHTTPError renamed SdkHttpError; HTTP status moves from .code to .status
breaking_changes.description: StreamableHTTPError becomes SdkHttpError (extending SdkError), and the HTTP status is read from .status instead of .code.
breaking_changes.migration_note: Rename the class and switch .code → .status in every check. .code on the old class held the HTTP status, which collided with JSON-RPC error codes — hence the rename.

breaking_changes.title: OAuth error classes consolidated into OAuthError + OAuthErrorCode
breaking_changes.description: The ~12 individual OAuth error classes (InvalidRequestError, InvalidClientError, InvalidGrantError, UnauthorizedClientError, …) are replaced by a single OAuthError class discriminated by OAuthErrorCode.
breaking_changes.migration_note: Replace each instanceof check on a specific OAuth error class with instanceof OAuthError plus a code comparison against the matching OAuthErrorCode member.

breaking_changes.title: SSEServerTransport and WebSocketClientTransport removed
breaking_changes.description: The deprecated HTTP+SSE server transport and the WebSocket client transport are removed from v2. A frozen v1 copy of the SSE transport is published at @modelcontextprotocol/server-legacy/sse for servers that must keep supporting legacy SSE clients.
breaking_changes.migration_note: Migrate SSE deployments to Streamable HTTP. If legacy SSE clients must still be served, depend on @modelcontextprotocol/server-legacy — but treat it as frozen, unmaintained code. There is no v2 WebSocket client transport.

breaking_changes.title: StreamableHTTPServerTransport split by runtime (Node vs web-standard)
breaking_changes.description: StreamableHTTPServerTransport is replaced by NodeStreamableHTTPServerTransport (from @modelcontextprotocol/node, for Node's http module) and WebStandardStreamableHTTPServerTransport (from @modelcontextprotocol/server, for web-standard Request/Response runtimes). For HTTP servers, createMcpHandler from @modelcontextprotocol/server is the new high-level entry point.
breaking_changes.before_code:
import { StreamableHTTPServerTransport } from '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/streamableHttp.js';

const transport = new StreamableHTTPServerTransport({ sessionIdGenerator: () => randomUUID() });
breaking_changes.after_code:
import { NodeStreamableHTTPServerTransport } from '@modelcontextprotocol/node';

const transport = new NodeStreamableHTTPServerTransport({ sessionIdGenerator: () => randomUUID() });
breaking_changes.migration_note: Pick the transport matching your runtime: @modelcontextprotocol/node for Node http, @modelcontextprotocol/server's WebStandardStreamableHTTPServerTransport for fetch-style runtimes, or the express/hono/fastify adapter packages. Upgrading the transport does NOT change the wire protocol — v2 still speaks the 2025-era protocol (sessions, initialize) unless you explicitly opt in to 2026-07-28 via createMcpHandler(factory)/serveStdio(buildServer).

breaking_changes.title: Wire-schema constants move from sdk/types.js to @modelcontextprotocol/core
breaking_changes.description: All Zod wire-schema constants (CallToolResultSchema, InitializeRequestSchema, …) move from @modelcontextprotocol/sdk/types.js to the @modelcontextprotocol/core package.
breaking_changes.migration_note: Add @modelcontextprotocol/core as a dependency wherever wire schemas are imported directly. Note many uses disappear entirely because setRequestHandler and client.request() no longer take schema arguments. Zod-free alternatives isSpecType.X(value) and specTypeSchemas are exported from the client/server packages.

breaking_changes.title: Header access is web-standard Headers — bracket access removed
breaking_changes.description: IsomorphicHeaders (plain-object headers with bracket access) is replaced by the web-standard Headers class on ctx.http.req.
breaking_changes.migration_note: Replace bracket access with .get(name) (case-insensitive). URL/query parsing goes through new URL(ctx.http.req.url).

breaking_changes.title: Result-schema argument dropped from client.request()/sendRequest() for spec methods
breaking_changes.description: For spec-defined methods, the result-schema second argument to client.request(), client.callTool(), and ctx.mcpReq.send() is removed — the SDK knows the result shape from the method string.
breaking_changes.migration_note: Delete the schema argument for spec methods. Custom methods keep explicit schemas via the 3-arg setRequestHandler form on the receiving side.

breaking_changes.title: completable() nesting inverted for optional schemas
breaking_changes.description: Optional completable prompt arguments invert: the completable wraps the base schema and .optional() is applied to the result, not the input.
breaking_changes.migration_note: Move .optional() from the inner schema to the completable(...) result.

breaking_changes.title: list*() client methods auto-aggregate paginated results
breaking_changes.description: listTools(), listResources(), etc. now follow pagination cursors automatically and return the aggregated list, up to listMaxPages (default 100). Exceeding the cap throws SdkError with SdkErrorCode.ListPaginationExceeded.
breaking_changes.after_code:
// v2: aggregation is automatic
const { tools } = await client.listTools();
breaking_changes.migration_note: Delete manual cursor loops — they would now iterate over already-aggregated results. Set listMaxPages if servers may expose more than 100 pages, and handle SdkErrorCode.ListPaginationExceeded.

breaking_changes.title: Custom Accept headers are appended, not replaced
breaking_changes.description: StreamableHTTPClientTransport now appends custom Accept values from requestInit.headers to the protocol-required media types (application/json, text/event-stream) instead of letting them replace the header.
breaking_changes.before_code:
// v1: this REPLACED the Accept header entirely
new StreamableHTTPClientTransport(url, { requestInit: { headers: { Accept: 'custom/type' } } });
breaking_changes.after_code:
// v2: same code now sends
// Accept: application/json, text/event-stream, custom/type
breaking_changes.migration_note: Code that relied on overriding Accept (e.g. to suppress SSE) no longer can; servers that dispatch on an exact Accept value may behave differently after upgrade.

breaking_changes.title: Experimental tasks (SEP-2663) removed
breaking_changes.description: The experimental task API is removed: extra.taskStore, extra.taskId, extra.taskRequestedTtl, and the task-handler schema constants (GetTaskRequestSchema, …).
breaking_changes.migration_note: There is no v2 equivalent in the beta; Tasks land as part of the 2026-07-28 spec revision. Servers using experimental tasks should stay on v1.x until the stable v2 tasks API ships.

breaking_changes.title: OAuth client hardening: TLS-only token endpoints, issuer binding, finishAuth(params)
breaking_changes.description: Several security behavior changes: (1) exchangeAuthorization()/refreshAuthorization()/fetchToken() throw InsecureTokenEndpointError for non-https token endpoints (loopback exempt); (2) they also throw OAuthError when the AS returns HTTP 200 with an {error} JSON body; (3) credentials are stamped with an issuer field and enforced per-AS (SEP-2352); (4) transport.finishAuth() takes the full callback URLSearchParams to read code + iss for RFC 9207 mix-up defense; (5) StreamableHTTPClientTransport gains onInsufficientScope ('reauthorize' default | 'throw') for 403 scope step-up (SEP-2350).
breaking_changes.migration_note: Pass the full callback searchParams to finishAuth(). Implement the optional discoveryState()/saveDiscoveryState() methods on OAuthClientProvider for issuer binding. Plain-http token endpoints (except localhost/127.0.0.1/::1) now hard-fail. New error classes to handle: InsecureTokenEndpointError, IssuerMismatchError, InsufficientScopeError, AuthorizationServerMismatchError.

breaking_changes.title: Removed Zod compat helpers and misc types
breaking_changes.description: Removed: schemaToJson (use z.toJSONSchema() or fromJsonSchema()), parseSchemaAsync (use the schema library's .safeParseAsync()), getSchemaShape(), getSchemaDescription(), isOptionalSchema(), unwrapOptionalSchema(), AnySchema, SchemaOutput, toJsonSchemaCompat, plus Protocol and mergeCapabilities from shared/protocol.js. SchemaInput<T> is replaced by StandardSchemaWithJSON.InferInput<T>. The ResourceTemplate TYPE (wire type) is renamed ResourceTemplateType; the ResourceTemplate CLASS is unchanged.
breaking_changes.before_code:
import { schemaToJson } from '@modelcontextprotocol/sdk/server/zod-compat.js';
const json = schemaToJson(mySchema);
breaking_changes.after_code:
import * as z from 'zod';
const json = z.toJSONSchema(mySchema);
breaking_changes.migration_note: The codemod rewrites SchemaInput<T> automatically. Watch the ResourceTemplate name collision: type-position uses become ResourceTemplateType, value/class uses stay ResourceTemplate.

deprecations.removal_timeline: The server→client sampling channel is eliminated in the 2026-07-28 protocol revision; handlers there return inputRequired(...) instead.
deprecations.note: Marked @deprecated in v2 but still functional on the 2025-era protocol.

deprecations.removal_timeline: The roots capability is deprecated by the 2026-07-28 protocol revision.
deprecations.note: Marked @deprecated in v2 but still functional on the 2025-era protocol.

deprecations.removal_timeline: The logging capability is deprecated by the 2026-07-28 protocol revision.
deprecations.note: Marked @deprecated in v2; ctx.mcpReq.log() is likewise deprecated.

deprecations.removal_timeline: The logging capability is deprecated by the 2026-07-28 protocol revision.
The roots capability is deprecated by the 2026-07-28 protocol revision.

deprecations.note: On 2026-07-28, handlers return inputRequired({ inputRequests: ... }) and read responses on retry via acceptedContent(ctx.mcpReq.inputResponses, id).

deprecations.replacement: inputRequired(...) return-value pattern for input; no logging replacement
deprecations.removal_timeline: These server→client request channels do not exist on the 2026-07-28 protocol revision.
deprecations.note: Declaring them is deprecated in v2 even on the 2025-era protocol.
Marked @deprecated in v2; throws RegistrationRejectedError with status/body/submittedMetadata on rejection.

deprecations.symbol: Raw Zod shape overloads of registerTool()/registerPrompt()
deprecations.removal_timeline: Deprecated overloads slated for removal in a later v2 release.

compatible_with.note: v2 depends on zod ^4.2.0. Zod v3 installs alongside the SDK without error but its schemas fail silently at tool registration.
